items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

children.content: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

gallery.altText: An artist impression of a modern, triangular-shaped building, surrounded by a pond.

thumbnail.altText: An artist impression of a modern, triangular-shaped building, surrounded by a pond.

items.title: Push to invest in early intervention as calls to police surge

children.content: A review of Tasmania Police finds calls for help have risen by 40,000 annually over the past decade, fuelling demands for investment in early intervention support services.

image.altText: A close-up of police hats on laps as police officer sit at a memorial service.

items.title: Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' developer's company in liquidation with unpaid super, millions in debts

children.content: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a round silver stadium jutting into the water, surrounded by green space, with Hobart city behind.

items.title: The typical night out is changing. Some say $15 pints are to blame

children.content: This weekend will be the first since the sudden closure of seven Hobart venues. A manager of another bar says he doesn't know what to expect.

gallery.altText: Woman drinking a beer whilst sitting and holding a cigarette

thumbnail.altText: Woman drinking a beer whilst sitting and holding a cigarette

items.title: The World Cup just had one of its hottest matches

children.content: A 38-degree Celsius evening match has raised fresh questions about how football manages heat in a warming world.

image.altText: Several Norwegian soccer players looking very hot and sweaty

images.altText: Mikel Merino drops to his knees and roars in celebration
Fabian Ruiz celebrates a goal by putting a ball under his shirt
Charles De Ketelaere heads the ball under pressure from Marc Cucurella

children.content: Spain will face France in a World Cup semifinal after beating Belgium 2-1 thanks to a late Mikel Merino goal. Follow live.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a man with sandy brown hair and clear plastic glasses smiling at the camera. Blue collared shirt.

items.title: Spain strikes late to seal semi berth after Belgium keeper howler

updates.title: FT: Late Merino strike sends Spain through

items.title: 'Key perpetrator' of spreading antisemitism draws ire of royal commission

children.content: It was gloves off at the royal commission on antisemitism this week, with Elon Musk's X bearing the brunt of the inquiry's frustrations with obstructive social media giants.

image.altText: A YouTube female executive on the left, a Meta executive in the middle, and Elon Musk on the right in a graphic.

items.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to wildlife haven behind tale of 'Storm Boy'

children.content: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

image.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.

items.title: Hinch revealed a scenario that seemed too crazy to be true — but he was right

children.content: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

image.altText: A man with a beard and glasses, photographed from below

items.title: A 'well-known' risk caused Telstra's outage. Why did it get caught out?

children.content: Telstra faces an outage investigation as an expert says legal gaps may let the telco off the hook.

image.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

items.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed days before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final days, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

thumbnail.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

items.title: King and Queen host Harry, Meghan and children at private residence

children.content: The royal couple hosted the duke and duchess and their children Archie and Lilibet at a country retreat.

image.altText: Meghan Markle cries as she stands next to Prince Harry, behind Queen Camilla and King Charles

items.title: How Jessica Mauboy is marking NAIDOC Week with her daughter

children.content: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

image.altText: Jess Mauboy against a teal background with her hands behind her head looking up, her rainbow fringed top flying in an arc

items.title: Hollywood's biggest names can't say no to Nolan

children.content: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Jurassic Park, the Scottish play and Wimbledon fruit

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Story of family 'terrorised by bogeyman' gets modern update in Cape Fear

children.content: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

image.altText: A TV still of Spanish actor Javier Bardem, 57, bearded, leans forward in dim light, with a sinister smile on his face.

items.title: Sinner, Zverev to face off in blockbuster Wimbledon final

children.content: British wildcard Arthur Fery's bid to make Wimbledon history has ended after he was knocked out by Alexander Zverev, who will face off against reigning champion Jannik Sinner in the final.

articles.title: Hewitt qualifies for Wimbledon boys' final, first Aussie since de Minaur

children.content: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

image.altText: Cruz Hewitt raises his left hand up in the air

gallery.altText: Jannik Sinner looks determined as he walks across court at Wimbledon.

thumbnail.altText: Jannik Sinner looks determined as he walks across court at Wimbledon.

items.title: Sports club ordered to redraw raffle after committee member wins $70k prize

children.content: A regional Victorian sports club has been ordered to redraw a fundraising raffle after an ineligible committee member won first prize, a $70,000 ute.

image.altText: The entry gate of a football club ground

items.title: 'Incredible musician': Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies

children.content: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

image.altText: A man playing a saxophone on a stage

items.title: The 99-year-old Victorian mechanic still working six days a week

image.altText: Close-up portrait of smiling elderly gentleman, wearing blue sweater and plaid collared shirt, before shelf with car bits

children.content: Ray Gloster has been a mechanic for 83 years and counting, and at 99, he might be on the verge of a world record.

items.title: Handmade quilts babies 'will have for life' wrap them in culture

children.content: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

articles.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

image.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

articles.title: Indigenous-led whale tour shares thousands of years of observations and knowledge

children.content: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.

image.altText: Two large whales breach over the water and are captured mid air.

gallery.altText: An Indigenous baby smiling in his mum's arms, with a brightly coloured patchwork quilt.

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous baby smiling in his mum's arms, with a brightly coloured patchwork quilt.

items.title: Workers waiting a year for entitlements from GFG may never be paid

children.content: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

image.altText: A man in a yellow high vis shirt and a beany both of which say Ferretti International.

items.title: Teenager arrested after remains found during search for missing girl

children.content: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

image.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: Outback towns welcome grey nomads after slow start to tourism season

children.content: Grey nomads are giving a much-needed boost to outback Queensland towns over the winter tourism season, but locals say more visitors are needed to fuel economic growth.

image.altText: Two grey nomads stand in front of a caravan in Mount Isa and smile. They have their arms around each other.

items.title: Grandmother's 32-hour bus 'adventure' home after train shutdown

children.content: A bus trip that should have taken only 13 hours turned into a 32-hour interstate marathon for a Torquay grandmother due to this week's Telstra outage, while there is still no date for when Victorian passengers can claim compensation from V/Line.

image.altText: A woman with blond hair smiles with a regional bus behind her.

items.title: Adrenaline junkie 89yo skydives to celebrate his birthday

children.content: Donald isn't alone in chasing thrills at an older age, with data from SkyDive Australia showing more than 14,000 people over 50 have dived since 2023.

image.altText: an older man looks to the camera for a selfie while skydiving

gallery.altText: a selfie of an older man skydiving, he is grinning into the camera

thumbnail.altText: a selfie of an older man skydiving, he is grinning into the camera

items.title: 'Healing from trauma': The fashion show not about the dresses

children.content: Esperance Nyirabarahinyuza survived the Rwandan genocide. Now, she is helping more than 100 refugee and migrant women in Logan rediscover confidence, community and belonging, one conversation at a time.

image.altText: a woman walks down a fashion runway in traditional african clothing

items.title: Belgian rider claims Stage 7 of Tour de France as Pogačar retains lead

children.content: Tim Merlier excels in the sprint stage of the Tour de France as race leader Tadej Pogačar keeps his closest rivals at bay.

image.altText: Tim Merlier raises his right fist in celebration

items.title: Stars of movies Moana and Supergirl came from same Sydney high school

children.content: Some cities have one hometown hero, but Newtown has two making the community proud in a double-feature of blockbuster hits you could watch on the same day.

gallery.altText: A composite of Lagaʻaia smiling in a headshot with Alcock as Supergirl smiling as she floats in the sky.

thumbnail.altText: A composite of Lagaʻaia smiling in a headshot with Alcock as Supergirl smiling as she floats in the sky.
